seoTitle: Wilsons Creek vs Wimbledon — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Wilsons Creek and Wimbledon. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Wilsons Creek vs Wimbledon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wilsons Creek and Wimbledon.
heading: Wilsons Creek vs Wimbledon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wilsons Creek, New South Wales and Wimbledon, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wimbledon has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Wilsons Creek (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wilsons Creek or Wimbledon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wilsons Creek has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Wimbledon scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wilsons Creek scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Wimbledon scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wilsons Creek or Wimbledon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wimbledon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
